frame_analyses.description: Title slide: 'WE TRAIN SIMULATION' with logo and course branding in Spanish. Introductory/instructional material.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: No task performance occurring — instructional context established.

frame_analyses.description: Slide showing gauze with circular marking and caption 'Cut through the marked line'. Shows the target pattern.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Teaching objective clearly communicated — trainees shown the circular pattern to be cut.

frame_analyses.description: Slide: 'CUTTING EXERCISES' title. Skill development framework presented.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content — skill progression structure being explained.

frame_analyses.description: Slide: 'Skill develop: Eye-hand Coordination'. Core competency being highlighted.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Emphasizes eye-hand coordination as essential cutting skill.

frame_analyses.description: Slide: 'Deepness perception'. Depth perception identified as critical skill for cutting task.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Teaching point: depth perception under laparoscopic magnification.

frame_analyses.description: Slide: 'Tissue manipulation'. Proper tissue handling technique component identified.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Tissue control emphasized as prerequisite for precision cutting.

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic trainer box shown in close-up. Equipment setup with two fixation clamps visible on white trainer base.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: MT-Box standard trainer visible. Clamp positioning shown.

frame_analyses.description: Overhead view of trainer. Two alligator clamps with strings securing gauze in horizontal position. White fixation base clearly visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard MT-Box setup with dual clamp configuration. Instructional view of equipment.

frame_analyses.description: Manual demonstration: hand showing technique to switch clamps from horizontal to vertical position using thumb/finger manipulation.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Teaching moment: clamp orientation adjustment — shows how to transition clamps for better gauge access.

frame_analyses.description: Close-up of blue gauze insert showing pre-marked circular pattern outline. Single-layer circle visible on blue substrate.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Marked circle pattern clearly visible. Heart-shaped pattern shown (appears to be practice/variant). Marking is dark blue, easily visible.

frame_analyses.description: Handheld view of MT-Box with blue gauze carrier insert in place. Two fixation pegs visible at top corners. Single circular pattern marked on surface.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper seating of gauze insert in trainer shown. Positioning for camera setup indicated.

frame_analyses.description: Slide: 'Close the MT-Box 1' with image showing closure mechanism of laparoscopic trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Equipment setup instruction — shows proper closure of trainer for video recording.

frame_analyses.description: Slide: 'Set the camera with your computer. And you are ready to go'. Final setup instruction slide.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Camera and recording setup instructions provided. No actual performance yet.

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic view begins: pink/magenta gauze visible with white backing layer. Marked circle outline visible in upper portion. Instrument (Maryland dissector) visible at bottom.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Live laparoscopic camera feed now active. Task initiation point — gauze being accessed via endoscope.

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic view: scissors visible positioning at gauze edge. Maryland dissector providing traction on gauze. Pink layer elevated, ready for cutting.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Scissors and dissector both in view. Entry cut phase — instrument setup showing proper two-hand technique with traction and cutting instruments.

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic view: scissors actively cutting along marked circular path. The cut line appears to follow the marked pattern. Maryland dissector maintains traction.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Scissors tracking circular pattern. Both hands coordinating — one holding traction, one cutting. Technique demonstrating proper two-hand coordination.

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic view: continuing circular cut. Scissors visible following marked line. Cut progressing clockwise around circle. Traction maintained on gauze.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth scissor movement. Maintaining visual alignment with marked circle. No obvious deviation visible from this camera angle.

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic view: nearly complete circular cut. Small segment remaining to complete the circle. Scissors approaching closure point of the circle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cut is nearly complete. Final segment visible. Circle is being freed from surrounding gauze. Technique remains controlled.

frame_analyses.description: Endoscopic view: marked circle appears completely detached from surrounding gauze. Circle is freed. Scissors repositioning, dissector holding freed circle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion achieved. The marked circle has been completely excised from the gauze. Cut appears clean.

frame_analyses.description: Slide: 'We repeat the exercise taking the time Each-time'. Instructional message about repetitive practice.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Reinforcement of deliberate practice approach. This is educational content, not continued task performance.

frame_analyses.description: Slide: 'We improve in every practice'. Motivational message concluding the instructional video.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final teaching reinforcement — emphasizes continuous improvement through repetition.

frame_analyses.description: Final slide: WE TRAIN SIMULATION branding, course title in Spanish, contact information. Video concluding.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: No performance task visible. Educational video ending.

video_classification_rationale: This video is classified as INSTRUCTIONAL. It contains: (1) Multiple title and explanatory slides with text overlays; (2) Equipment setup demonstrations with voiceless instructional captions; (3) Skill development framework slides identifying core competencies; (4) Final motivational/reinforcement messages. The brief laparoscopic cutting sequence (frames 14–19) is presented as a DEMONSTRATION of the task within an educational context, not as a formal trainee performance to be scored. The video's primary purpose is teaching the task procedure and setup, not evaluating a trainee's technique.

instructional_classification_details.narration_style: Text-overlay slide format with instructional captions
instructional_classification_details.content_type: Educational tutorial and equipment setup guide
expert_demo_indicators: Proper traction and cutting instrument use shown
Marked circle successfully cut and freed

cannot_score_reason: This is an instructional video, not a formal performance attempt. No timing data is provided, and the brief cutting sequence shown is a teaching demonstration, not a scored trainee performance.

demonstrated_techniques: Two-hand coordination: dissector providing traction while scissors cut
Scissor positioning for entry cut from gauze edge
Following a marked circular path with continuous cutting motion
Maintaining traction while advancing the cutting tool
Complete circle release and separation from surrounding gauze
MT-Box trainer setup and orientation adjustment (clamp positioning)
Endoscopic camera setup for task recording
verbal_cues: Switch the clamps to vertical position (for optimal access)
Use traction and contraction (demonstrated via image with caption)
Repeat the exercise taking the time each-time (practice reinforcement)
We improve in every practice (motivational message)
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding deviation from the marked circle outline — video shows precision tracking
Proper two-hand technique: one hand retracts, one cuts (explicitly shown)
Maintaining tissue control during cutting (dissector tension maintained throughout)
Equipment positioning: clamp orientation adjustment for better visualization
Incomplete cuts: video shows full circle release as required endpoint
teaching_content.skill_level_justification: The demonstration shows smooth, controlled cutting with consistent pattern following, proper two-hand coordination, and clean circle release. Completion appears rapid and controlled, consistent with intermediate-expert performance benchmarks (60–100s).

frame_deviation_analysis.observable_pattern_tracking: Cut path appears to follow marked circle outline with minimal visible deviation. Circle is cleanly separated from surrounding gauze.
frame_deviation_analysis.reason: Laparoscopic camera angle is oblique and zoomed for instructional purposes. Final product is not shown in high detail. Cannot measure cardinal-point deviations per rubric without final specimen inspection.

score_components.reasoning: This is an instructional/educational video, not a formal trainee performance. Official FLS scoring rubric applies only to actual performance attempts with measurable completion times and identifiable penalties. This video demonstrates task technique within an educational framework.

confidence_rationale: Very high confidence in video classification. The instructional intent is explicit: multiple teaching slides, equipment setup sequences, skill framework slides, and motivational messaging. The brief cutting demonstration is clearly presented as a teaching example, not a timed, scored performance. No ambiguity in classification.
summary: This is an instructional/educational video from 'We Train Simulation' (WeTrainSimulation.com) demonstrating FLS Task 2 (Pattern Cut) setup and technique execution. The video covers equipment preparation, skill development framework (eye-hand coordination, depth perception, tissue manipulation), proper MT-Box trainer orientation, and a brief endoscopic demonstration of a complete circular cut. The teaching content is comprehensive and emphasizes deliberate repetitive practice. Per scoring guidelines, instructional videos are not formally scored — only actual trainee performances are evaluated.
